questions.question:
Additional steps to apply fix pack to Decision Center 

We are upgrading ODM 8.8.1 Decision Center to the latest 8.8.1.3.

Are there any additional steps to apply the fixpack to Decision Center after we install the fixpack with Installation Manager?
questions.expected_answer_text: If you have installed interim fixes for Operational Decision Manager V8.5.1, you must uninstall the fixes before you can install this fix pack.
questions.expected_document_title: [techqa] DOWNLOADABLE FILES ABSTRACT This cumulative fix pack
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: If you have installed interim fixes for Operational Decision Manager V8.5.1, you must uninstall the fixes before you can install this fix pack.
questions.source_chunk_content:
After installing the fixpack with the Installation Manager, you may need to perform additional steps to apply the fix pack to Decision Center:

1. Check if any interim fixes were previously installed for Operational Decision Manager V8.8.1. If so, make sure to uninstall these fixes before installing the latest fix pack. Failure to do so may cause issues during the installation of the fix pack.

2. Stop the Operational Decision Manager sample server before installing the fix pack to ensure a smooth installation process.

3. If you had disconnected any rule projects from Decision Center in Rule Designer, make sure to restore the connection for the dependent projects before applying the fix pack. It is recommended to keep the rule projects connected to Decision Center during the synchronization process.

By following these additional steps, you can successfully apply the fix pack to Decision Center and ensure the stability and functionality of your ODM 8.8.1 system.

questions.question: Any tips for off-road driving?
questions.expected_answer_text: If you get hung up or high-centered on an object, get out of the vehicle and try to determine what the vehicle is hung up on, where it is contacting the underbody and what is the best direction to recover the vehicle.
questions.expected_document_title: [delucionqa] If you get hung up or high-centered
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: If you get hung up or high-centered on an object, get out of the vehicle and try to determine what the vehicle is hung up on, where it is contacting the underbody and what is the best direction to recover the vehicle.
questions.source_chunk_content:
Some tips for off-road driving mentioned in the context include: 
1. Always be familiar with the terrain and area before proceeding.
2. Maintain a firm grip on the steering wheel and good driving posture.
3. Avoid sudden accelerations, turns, or braking.
4. Always be looking ahead for surface obstacles and changes in terrain.
5. Disable the Stop/Start system during off-road use.
6. Always wear your seat belt and firmly tie down cargo to avoid projectiles in an off-road situation.
7. Never park your vehicle over dry grass or other combustible materials.
8. If stuck or high-centered on an object, try to determine what the vehicle is hung up on, where it is contacting the underbody, and the best direction to recover the vehicle.
9. Before climbing hills, consider the grade, traction, obstacles, and your ability to safely recover the vehicle.
10. When approaching large rocks, choose a path that allows your tires to drive over them to protect your undercarriage.

questions.question: What is the structure of Hantaan virus?
questions.expected_answer_text: Passage: negative polarity: S segment codes for the nucleocapsid protein and a small non-structural s protein in several viral species; M segment for the glycoprotein precursor of the envelope glycoproteins Gn and Gc; and L segment for the RNA-dependent RNA polymerase .

questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Passage: negative polarity: S segment codes for the nucleocapsid protein and a small non-structural s protein in several viral species; M segment for the glycoprotein precursor of the envelope glycoproteins Gn and Gc; and L segment for the RNA-dependent RNA polymerase .
questions.source_chunk_content: The structure of Hantaan virus is spherical or ovoid, composed of 20-30% fat, >50% protein, 7% carbohydrates, and 2% RNA. The genome comprises three negative sense, single-stranded RNAs that consist of the small, medium, and large segments encoding the nucleoprotein, envelope glycoproteins, and viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase, respectively. The outer membrane of Hantaan virus is composed of Gn and Gc glycoproteins.

questions.source_chunk_content:
Based on the provided context, the issue of App Connect Professional Studio crashing without any error message or warning could be caused by a problem with the heap memory allocation for the Studio application. 

To resolve this issue, you can try increasing the size of the heap memory allocated to the Cast Iron Studio by following these steps:

1. Start the Studio through the command line interface using the following command:
   ```
   CastIronStudio.exe -J-Xmx1024m
   ```
   In this command, "1024" represents the new memory size that will be allocated to the Studio instead of the default value of 512. This numeric value is in MB.
   
2. If increasing the heap memory size does not resolve the issue, you may need to open a service request with IBM Cast Iron Support for further assistance.

You can also try using garbage collection from Studio to free up unused space and check the XML Schema preference settings to ensure that the memory usage is within acceptable limits.

questions.source_chunk_content:
To calculate the percentage change in revenue from 2018 to 2019, we can use the following formula:

Percentage Change = ((Revenue 2019 - Revenue 2018) / Revenue 2018) * 100

Using the revenue numbers provided in the context:
Revenue 2018 = US$140,368,000
Revenue 2019 = US$172,752,000

Percentage Change = ((172,752,000 - 140,368,000) / 140,368,000) * 100
Percentage Change = (32,384,000 / 140,368,000) * 100
Percentage Change = 0.2306 * 100
Percentage Change = 23.06%

Therefore, the percentage change in revenue from 2018 to 2019 is 23.06%.

This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.

questions.question: How to properly re-install the carpets in the car?
questions.expected_answer_text: Front and rear carpet split under the back of the front seat, open the carpet split and then pull out the rear edge and slide the carpet to the front (do not remove the harness).
questions.expected_document_title: [delucionqa] Carpet Safety Information Always use carpet designed
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: Front and rear carpet split under the back of the front seat, open the carpet split and then pull out the rear edge and slide the carpet to the front (do not remove the harness).
questions.source_chunk_content:
To properly re-install the carpets in the car, follow these steps:

1. For the front carpets: 
- Remove the front grommets and pull the front carpet out from the front to the rear.
- Remove the grommets under the front seat.
- Starting with the rear carpet, then the front carpet, split them under the back of the front seat.
- Open the carpet split, pull out the rear edge, and slide the carpet to the front (do not remove the harness).
- Finally, open the carpet split around the seat bracket and remove the last two grommets.
- When reinstalling, tuck the carpet under the scuffs, B-pillar, and console, then refasten the grommets.

2. For the rear carpet:
- Remove the grommets under the front seat (one left and one right).
- Split the carpet and pull it out to the rear, then open the carpet split around the front seat brackets.
- Remove the rear Under Seat storage bin (if equipped) and the jack and tools.
- Lift the carpet off the rear retainers (one left and one right).
- Finally, remove the carpet from the vehicle.

When reinstalling the rear carpet, perform the steps in reverse order, making sure to tuck the carpet under the scuffs, B-pillar, console, and refasten the grommets.

questions.question: Why SSH connections fail after upgrade to v7.5.2 and above? Why SSH connections fail after upgrade to v7.5.2 and above? The same configuration works on v7.2.
questions.expected_answer_text: If the SSH client/SSH server is older and does not yet support the safer ciphers and MACs, and wants to use one of the unsafe ciphers/MACs disabled by default, the connection will fail.
questions.expected_document_title: [techqa] TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING) PROBLEM(ABSTRACT) Applying PTFs to 5733SC1
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: If the SSH client/SSH server is older and does not yet support the safer ciphers and MACs, and wants to use one of the unsafe ciphers/MACs disabled by default, the connection will fail.
questions.source_chunk_content: SSH connections fail after upgrading to v7.5.2 and above because the default set of ciphers and MACs have been altered in OpenSSH releases starting from version 6.7. Unsafe algorithms such as CBC ciphers and arcfour are disabled by default in these versions. If the SSH client or server being used is older and does not support the safer algorithms enabled by default in v7.5.2 and above, the connection will fail. To resolve this issue, either upgrade the SSH client/server to a version that supports the safe algorithms or modify the OpenSSH configuration file to add back the unsafe algorithms temporarily.

questions.question:
Why does nco_confpack -export fail with the message "Tool in menu but not found"? 

I tried to export OMNIbus configuration using nco_confpack command and I get the following message:

Tool in menu but not found, menu: AlertsMenu, tool: Close

I've also tried using it with -list command and I get the same error.

Why is this happening?
questions.expected_answer_text: The command fails and the following error is received: Tool in menu but not found, menu: AlertsMenu, tool: CLEAR_LWY. CAUSE
questions.expected_document_title: [techqa] tool; menu; not found; confpack TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: The command fails and the following error is received: Tool in menu but not found, menu: AlertsMenu, tool: CLEAR_LWY. CAUSE
questions.source_chunk_content: The error "Tool in menu but not found" occurs because the menu AlertsMenu contains a tool called Close that no longer exists. The nco_confpack command is trying to export the configuration which includes this tool but since it is not found, the export fails. To resolve this issue, the tool Close needs to be recreated and associated with the menu AlertsMenu before trying to run the nco_confpack command again for exporting the configuration successfully.

To remove the Tonneau Cover, follow these steps:
1. Open the tailgate.
2. Fold the rear panel of the Tonneau Cover forward to reveal the red release straps.
3. Pull one of the red release straps to release the tonneau cover latch.
4. Roll the Tonneau Cover forward, starting with the rear bar and continue to roll toward the front of the truck bed.
5. Roll tonneau cover forward using the stowage straps and secure the tonneau cover in the rolled up position.
6. Use a #t50 torx head driver to remove the two fasteners securing the tonneau cover to the front of the truck bed.
7. Utilizing two people, lift the tonneau cover up and away from the truck bed.
8. Store the Tonneau Cover in a safe location.

For example:

questions.expected_answer_text: http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24043227 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24043227] WebSphere Message Broker V8 IT16743 An interim fix is available from IBM Fix Central for all platforms http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/selectFixes?parent=ibm~WebSphere&product=ibm/WebSphere/WebSphere+Message+Broker&release=All&platform=All&function=aparId&apars=IT16743 [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/selectFixes?parent=ibm~WebSphere&product=ibm/WebSphere/WebSphere+Message+Broker&release=All&platform=All&function=aparId&apars=%20IT16743]

questions.expected_answer_excerpt: http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24043227 [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg24043227] WebSphere Message Broker V8 IT16743 An interim fix is available from IBM Fix Central for all platforms http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/selectFixes?parent=ibm~WebSphere&product=ibm/WebSphere/WebSphere+Message+Broker&release=All&platform=All&function=aparId&apars=IT16743 [http://www.ibm.com/support/fixcentral/swg/selectFixes?parent=ibm~WebSphere&product=ibm/WebSphere/WebSphere+Message+Broker&release=All&platform=All&function=aparId&apars=%20IT16743]
questions.source_chunk_content:
When installing a fix pack for IBM Integration Bus (IIB) in a multi-instance environment, it is important to consider the following:

1. **Installation and Verification Tasks**: 
   - Ensure that the fix pack is compatible with your current version of IIB.
   - Follow the installation instructions provided by IBM for applying the fix pack to a multi-instance environment.
   - Verify that the installation is successful on all instances of IIB in your environment.

2. **Rollback Procedure**: 
   - Before applying the fix pack, make sure to have a backup of your current configuration and data.
   - Understand the rollback procedure in case the fix pack causes any issues.
   - Test the rollback procedure in a non-production environment to ensure it works as expected.

3. **Multi-Instance Configuration Tasks**:
   - Ensure that all instances of IIB are stopped before applying the fix pack.
   - Apply the fix pack to each instance of IIB individually to avoid any issues related to multi-instance configuration.
   - After applying the fix pack to all instances, start them one by one to ensure proper functioning.

4. **Testing After Installation**:
   - Once the fix pack is applied to all instances, perform thorough testing of your applications to ensure that they are functioning correctly.
   - Test the communication and interaction between different instances of IIB to ensure that the multi-instance setup is still working as expected.

5. **Verify Post-Installation Configurations**:
   - Double-check any custom configurations or settings that were applied to the multi-instance setup to ensure they are still in place after the fix pack installation.
   - Verify that any integration points or dependencies with other systems are not affected by the fix pack installation.

By considering these aspects, you can ensure a smooth installation process of the fix pack for IBM Integration Bus in a multi-instance environment and minimize any potential issues that may arise.

questions.question: How can I replace the full doors with half doors?
questions.expected_answer_text: To install the front door exterior mirrors on the half-doors, proceed as follows: To install the front or rear half-doors, proceed as follows: Locate the service hole in the center of each plug of the lower half-door (two on each front and rear door).

questions.expected_answer_excerpt: To install the front door exterior mirrors on the half-doors, proceed as follows: To install the front or rear half-doors, proceed as follows: Locate the service hole in the center of each plug of the lower half-door (two on each front and rear door).
questions.source_chunk_content:
To replace the full doors with half doors on your vehicle, you will need to follow these steps:

1. Begin by removing the full doors from the vehicle. For detailed instructions on front and rear door removal, refer to the specific sections in the provided context.

2. Next, locate the upper and lower hinge pins on the lower half-door and lower them into the body hinges on the vehicle. Insert the hinge pin screws into the body hinges and tighten them using a #T50 Torx head driver to the specified torque (3.8 ft·lb / 5.2 N·m).

3. Attach the cloth strap of the lower half-door to the metal hook just inside the vehicle. Connect the wiring harness on the lower half-door to the connection inside the vehicle.

4. If the half-doors are shipped with plugs in the post holes, remove these plugs by following the provided instructions.

5. Insert the upper half-door into the lower half-door by placing the posts into the post holes. Push down firmly to fully seat the upper half-door.

6. Check that the upper half-doors are positioned properly to ensure proper sealing and prevent water leaks. Store the zipper pulls of the upper half-door windows at the upper B-pillar area when not in use.

7. Avoid running half-doors through an automatic car wash and handling or storing them carelessly to prevent damage to the seals and windows.

To reinstall the full doors, simply repeat the installation steps in reverse order. Make sure to adjust the doors correctly against the striker to prevent damage to both the door and the striker. Do not adjust the striker itself, as this may affect the positioning of the full door.

questions.question:
How do I resolve a WSVR0605W hung thread issue in the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter? The following WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter hung thread can prevent an application server from starting...

WSVR0605W: Thread "WMQJCAResourceAdapter : 6" (0000001b) has been active for 698530 milliseconds and may be hung. There is 1 thread(s) in total in the server that may be hung. at java.lang.Object.wait(Native Method) at java.lang.Object.wait(Object.java:167) at com.ibm.ejs.container.MessageEndpointFactoryImpl.createEndpoint(MessageEndpointFactoryImpl.java:498) at com.ibm.mq.connector.inbound.WorkImpl.run(WorkImpl.java:149)
questions.expected_answer_text: You can access the configuration for work managers in the administrative console under Resources > Asynchronous beans > Work managers > es-workmanager.
questions.expected_document_title: [techqa] WSVR0605W; WorkManager; es-workmanager; hanging threads; thread pool;
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: You can access the configuration for work managers in the administrative console under Resources > Asynchronous beans > Work managers > es-workmanager.
questions.source_chunk_content:
To resolve a WSVR0605W hung thread issue in the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter, you need to tune the thread pool size for the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter. By default, the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter thread pool is created with a minimum of 2 threads and a maximum of 20 threads. The Growable option is set to true, allowing the number of threads to go beyond the maximum if needed. To prevent blocked threads, you may need to limit and tune the maximum thread pool size. Individual performance tuning in a pre-production test environment is recommended to avoid performance problems on a production server.

You can access the configuration for work managers in the administrative console under Resources > Asynchronous beans > Work managers > WMQJCAResourceAdapter. It is important to configure work managers according to your specific requirements and workload to prevent hung thread issues.

To calculate the percentage change in the value of raw materials from 2018 to 2019, we first need to find the raw material values for both years.

In 2018, the value of raw materials was $2,969, and in 2019, the value was $3,445.

To calculate the percentage change, we use the formula:

Percentage Change = ((New Value - Old Value) / Old Value) * 100

Percentage Change = (($3,445 - $2,969) / $2,969) * 100
Percentage Change = ($476 / $2,969) * 100
Percentage Change ≈ 16.017%

Therefore, the percentage change in the value of raw materials from 2018 to 2019 is approximately 16.017%.

questions.question: What should I do to jump start the car?
questions.expected_answer_text: If your vehicle is equipped with a Stop/Start system, it will be equipped with two batteries See the following steps to prepare for jump starting: Apply the parking brake, shift the automatic transmission into PARK (P) (manual transmission in NEUTRAL) and place the ignition in OFF mode.
questions.expected_document_title: [delucionqa] Preparations For Jump Start The battery in
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: If your vehicle is equipped with a Stop/Start system, it will be equipped with two batteries See the following steps to prepare for jump starting: Apply the parking brake, shift the automatic transmission into PARK (P) (manual transmission in NEUTRAL) and place the ignition in OFF mode.
questions.source_chunk_content:
To jump start the car, you should follow these steps:

- Prepare for jump starting by applying the parking brake, shifting the transmission into PARK or NEUTRAL, turning off the ignition, and all electrical accessories.
- Make sure the vehicle providing the jump start is parked within reach of the jumper cables, with the ignition turned OFF.
- Only use the positive battery post on the main battery to jump start your vehicle.
- Avoid using a booster battery greater than 12 Volts.
- Ensure that cable clamps do not touch each other.
- Lift up the protective cap on the positive battery post to gain access.
- Do not allow vehicles to touch each other.
- Avoid the radiator cooling fan when the hood is raised.
- Remove any metal jewelry that could make inadvertent electrical contact.
- Keep open flames or sparks away from the battery.

After following these preparations, you can proceed with the jump starting procedure.

questions.question: What is the treatment for MERS-COV?
questions.expected_answer_text: One must cover their nose and mouth during instances of sneezing and coughing with a tissue and avoid touching the mouth, nose, or eyes with their hands until washed properly.
questions.expected_document_title: [covidqa] Title: Demographic Variations of MERS-CoV Infection among
questions.expected_answer_excerpt: One must cover their nose and mouth during instances of sneezing and coughing with a tissue and avoid touching the mouth, nose, or eyes with their hands until washed properly.
questions.source_chunk_content: The treatment for MERS-CoV is supportive and symptomatic, as there is currently no specific treatment or vaccine available. Supportive care, such as respiratory support, hydration, antipyretics, and infection control, is the mainstay of treatment for MERS-CoV. Additionally, preventative actions such as hand hygiene and covering the nose and mouth during sneezing and coughing are recommended. Various therapeutic options have been investigated, but no therapy of proven value currently exists.
